skill_name: Configure Default Search Engine And Search Preferences
overview: Use Chrome Search engine settings and Google Search Settings surfaces to change providers or inspect search-display preferences without inventing controls that are not visible.
when_to_use: Change the default search provider inside Chrome settings.
Inspect or modify search-engine entries and related provider management surfaces.
Open Google Search Settings surfaces when the task is about search-display behavior such as result layout or similar preferences.
preconditions: Chrome can open the Search engine settings surface.

atomic_capabilities.name: reach_surface
atomic_capabilities.purpose: Reach the relevant Chrome surface for configure default search engine and search preferences

atomic_capabilities.name: execute_and_verify
atomic_capabilities.purpose: Execute configure default search engine and search preferences and verify the requested result state

decision_guide.condition: The correct Chrome surface is not open yet.
decision_guide.reason: Surface errors cause benchmark failures before the core action can start.

decision_guide.condition: The main interaction already ran but completion is still uncertain.
decision_guide.reason: This skill is only complete when the requested visible result state is present.

when_to_use: change the default provider, manage search-engine entries, or inspect Google Search Settings surfaces when the task is about search-display behavior

states.visual_grounding: Relevant controls on Chrome Search engine settings and the downstream search-result page
states.trigger_condition: The workflow still needs to change the default provider, optionally delete extra engines, and verify downstream search preferences
states.action: Advance Chrome through the `open_search_engine_surface` subgoal until the requested search provider and preference filter are visibly active.
states.text_description: State `open_search_engine_surface` belongs to the Chrome workflow for change the default provider, optionally delete extra engines, and verify downstream search preferences.

key_frame.annotation_note: Open the Search engine section from the Settings sidebar.

highlight_targets.name: Search engine sidebar item
highlight_targets.annotation_query: Search engine item in the Chromium Settings left sidebar

states.visual_grounding: Relevant controls on Chrome Search engine settings and the downstream search-result page
states.trigger_condition: The workflow still needs to change the default provider, optionally delete extra engines, and verify downstream search preferences
states.action: Advance Chrome through the `set_provider_or_engine_list` subgoal until the requested search provider and preference filter are visibly active.
states.text_description: State `set_provider_or_engine_list` belongs to the Chrome workflow for change the default provider, optionally delete extra engines, and verify downstream search preferences.

highlight_targets.name: Set as default button
highlight_targets.annotation_query: Set as default button in the Search engine dialog

states.visual_grounding: Relevant controls on Chrome Search engine settings and the downstream search-result page
states.trigger_condition: The workflow still needs to change the default provider, optionally delete extra engines, and verify downstream search preferences
states.action: Advance Chrome through the `apply_result_preference` subgoal until the requested search provider and preference filter are visibly active.
states.text_description: State `apply_result_preference` belongs to the Chrome workflow for change the default provider, optionally delete extra engines, and verify downstream search preferences.

key_frame.annotation_note: Open the manage-search-engines surface from Search engine settings.

highlight_targets.name: Manage search engines row
highlight_targets.annotation_query: Manage search engines and site search row in Chromium Search engine settings

states.visual_grounding: Relevant controls on Chrome Search engine settings and the downstream search-result page
states.trigger_condition: The workflow still needs to change the default provider, optionally delete extra engines, and verify downstream search preferences
states.action: Advance Chrome through the `verify_provider_or_filter_state` subgoal until the requested search provider and preference filter are visibly active.
states.text_description: State `verify_provider_or_filter_state` belongs to the Chrome workflow for change the default provider, optionally delete extra engines, and verify downstream search preferences.

key_frame.annotation_note: Verify the downstream search-result preference is applied.

highlight_targets.name: Date filter showing Past 24 hours
highlight_targets.annotation_query: Past 24 hours option in the Date dropdown on Bing Images results

states.visual_grounding: Google search results page with the Search Settings menu open
states.trigger_condition: The task is about Google Search display preferences rather than Chrome's default provider and the results page is already visible.
states.action: Use the open Search Settings menu to enter the more detailed Google Search settings surface.
states.text_description: State `open_google_search_settings_entry` identifies the Search Settings menu path from live Google results.

key_frame.annotation_note: Open the deeper Google Search settings surface from the results-page menu.

highlight_targets.name: More settings tile
highlight_targets.annotation_query: More settings tile in the Google Search settings menu on the results page

states.visual_grounding: Google Search settings Other settings page with desktop toggles
states.trigger_condition: The task has already reached Google Search Settings and needs to inspect the currently visible desktop preference controls before claiming success.
states.action: Inspect the visible Google Search settings controls and confirm whether the requested preference is actually present before toggling anything or marking the task complete.
states.text_description: State `inspect_google_other_settings_surface` is the cautionary surface for Google Search settings when the requested control may be absent from the visible page.

key_frame.annotation_note: Inspect the actual controls visible on the Other settings page instead of inventing absent ones.

highlight_targets.name: Results in a new window toggle
highlight_targets.annotation_query: Results in a new window toggle on the Google Search settings Other settings page
